rationale: The cited paper directly examines how an initial in-store purchase trigger (surprise coupon on a planned item) increases unplanned purchases and basket spending, which closely overlaps with the citing paper’s core question about whether an initial purchase increases subsequent unrelated buying. The citing paper also tests and argues against a purely affect-based account, making the cited paper’s mood/psychological-income mechanisms a plausible theory/comparison source rather than a generic mention. Given this conceptual and mechanism-level overlap, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
